objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: mean_reversion_bollinger_rsi
archetype: mean_reversion_bollinger_rsi
family: mean_reversion
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- bollinger(period=15, std_dev=2.3)
- rsi(period=7)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: close < bollinger.lower and rsi < 40
- short: close > bollinger.upper and rsi > 70
exit:
- condition: cross_any(close, bollinger.middle) or cross_any(rsi, 50)
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 2.75
tp_atr_mult: 4.0
description: ATR stop-loss (2.75x) and take-profit (4.0x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

iterations.hypothesis: Using Bollinger Bands and RSI with ATR-based risk management for a mean reversion strategy in the 1h timeframe.

pre_reflection.text: [Pre-reflection] Iteration 1/10 of mean_reversion_bollinger_rsi strategy is running. The backtest results are not yet available. Potential weaknesses include potential overfitting due to the number of indicators used, limited exploration of other volatility measures and risk management strategies. If results are poor (negative return), adjusting stop-loss and take profit values can be explored. For mediocre performance (low Sharpe ratio) further investigation into improving RSI sensitivity or exploring mean reversion with different timeframes could be considered. Alternative approach would include trying more advanced machine learning techniques to predict price movements based on historical data.

iterations.hypothesis: La stratégie combine ATR pour mesurer la volatilité, les signaux RSI et Bollinger Bands pour détecter des zones de support/résistance, puis utilise l'ADX pour identifier le courant du marché. Les entrées sont permises lorsque le prix se trouve en hausse avec un signalfort alors qu'en sortie les entrées se font lorsqu'il y a une divergence entre la tendance et les signaux RSI ou Bollinger.
Volatility-based mean reversion strategy with RSI and ATR indicators.
